records.ety: [L. substitutus, p. p. of substituere to put under, put in the place of; sub under + statuere to put, place: cf. F. substitut. See Statute.]

defs: One who, or that which, is substituted or put in the place of another; one who acts for another; that which stands in lieu of something else
a person who enlists for military service in the place of a conscript or drafted man.
quotes: Hast thou not made me here thy substitute?
Ladies [in Shakespeare's age] . . . wore masks as the sole substitute known to our ancestors for the modern parasol.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Substituted (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Substituting (?).]

defs: To put in the place of another person or thing; to exchange.
quotes: Some few verses are inserted or substituted in the room of others.
